### 1. Total Concerts (1982–2024)
Iron Maiden is one of the most prolific touring acts in rock. Cataloguing major tours (Beast on the Road, World Slavery, Somewhere on Tour, Seventh Son tour, 90s tours, Brave New World, Dance of Death, Somewhere Back in Time, Final Frontier, Book of Souls, Legacy of the Beast, Future Past…):

**≈ 2,300 concerts**

### 2. Average Attendance (weighted across eras)
| Era | Shows | Avg. Attendance | Person-Attendances |
|-----|-------|----------------:|-------------------:|
| 1980s | ~800 | 10,000 | 8,000,000 |
| 1990s | ~400 | 8,000 | 3,200,000 |
| 2000s | ~400 | 12,000 | 4,800,000 |
| 2010s | ~400 | 15,000 | 6,000,000 |
| 2020s | ~300 | 15,000 | 4,500,000 |

**Total person-attendances ≈ 26,500,000**

### 3. Probability of Attending on One's Birthday
$$P = \frac{1}{365.25} \approx 0.00274$$

**Birthday attendees ≈ 26,500,000 / 365.25 ≈ 72,500**

### 4. Attendance-Weighted Average Age
The fanbase has aged with the band (young crowds in the 80s, increasingly older from 2000s onward), while new fans continually join. Weighting by decade attendance:

$$\bar{a} = \frac{8M \times 22 + 3.2M \times 26 + 4.8M \times 31 + 6M \times 36 + 4.5M \times 40}{26.5M} \approx 30 \text{ years}$$

## Final Calculation

$$72{,}500 \;\text{birthday attendees} \times 30.3 \;\text{avg. years} \approx 2{,}200{,}000$$
